---
title: "Data types HW 1"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



rm(list = ls())






















```{r}

matdata <- c("AA",-3.3,8.2,"bb",-1,10.5,"cc",1.3,12.8)
matex2 <- matrix(data = matdata, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)


```









```{r}
E1values <- c(163,3.6,10.30,.58,3.10)
E1datalabels <- c("Displacement","Net Power Out","Net Torque","Oil Cap","Fuel Tank")
#labels()
is.vector(E1values)
names(E1values) <- E1datalabels

attributes(E1values)
str(E1values)
dim(E1values)
nrow(E1values)
length(E1values)
```

```{r}
multe1 <- 3*E1values
```





```{r}
hwcol <- c("AA","BB","CC")
hwrow1 <- c(-3.3,-1.0,1.3)
hwrow2 <- c(8.2,10.5,12.8)


combinedv <- c(hwcol,hwrow1,hwrow2)
HW1mat <- matrix(data = combinedv, ncol =3)
str(HW1mat)
```
```{r}
bettermat <- rbind(hwcol,hwrow1,hwrow2)
```


```{r}
specificvalue <- bettermat[3,2]
```
